commands.review_summary:
This PR adds auto-login guidance to the macOS VM install docs in English and zh-CN, and adds an auto-login hint to the macOS LaunchAgent bootstrap error.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real reproduction is present. Source inspection shows the current docs gap and the generic LaunchAgent diagnostic path, but the PR body and checks do not include a real headless macOS VM failure or after-fix run.
commands.review_followup: Contributor proof and maintainer judgment on security-sensitive auto-login wording are required before merge, so this is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: improve macOS VM auto-login guidance for headless setup
target.body:
## Changes

- Add auto-login setup step in macOS VM documentation (English and Chinese)
- Improve error message in launchd.ts to suggest enabling auto-login

## Problem

When running OpenClaw in a headless macOS VM, users encounter LaunchAgent bootstrap failures because the system requires a logged-in GUI session. The error message didn't provide clear guidance on how to fix this.

## Solution

1. Added an extra post-setup step in the VM setup documentation to enable auto-login in System Settings
2. Enhanced the error message to explicitly mention the auto-login solution for headless/VM setups

This makes it easier for users to set up OpenClaw in headless macOS VMs without running into authentication issues.
target.author: xzcxzcyy-claw
